text:
BOTTOM LINE — Divergence tape: semiconductors dumped, small caps edged. The tape decoupled: growth cyclicals like XLC +1.73% and XLY +0.95% rallied while XLK -1.11% and SOXX -2.23% cracked, showing a rotation out of AI hardware into other risk segments. Dollar weakness (-0.49%) and falling VIX (15.7) amplified the broad risk-on tone underneath the headline slump.

INDICES — VIX 15.7, down 2.90%. Rank by return: IJR +0.54%, IWM +0.43%, SPY +0.40%, DIA +0.24%, QQQ -0.27%, SOXX -2.23%. Small > large by 0.14pp. Most notable cross-asset: USO oil +1.01% despite XLE -0.79%. Neither SPY nor QQQ is below the 200-day MA.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.8x with price up = low-conviction rally. QQQ vol_ratio 0.8x with price down = distribution lacks urgency. SOXX vol 1.1x with price -2.23% = confirmed distribution in semis. XLF vol 1.6x with price +0.68% = accumulation in financials.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLC Comms +1.73%, XLY Discretionary +0.95%, XLF Financials +0.68%, XLRE Real Estate +0.18%, XLP Staples +0.06%, XLV Healthcare +0.00%, XLI Industrials -0.22%, XLB Materials -0.28%, XLE Energy -0.79%, XLU Utilities -1.03%, XLK Technology -1.11%. Top Industries: KWEB +3.13%, MAGS +2.34%. Bottom: SOXX -2.23%, DRAM -6.26%. Money rotating out of XLK and SOXX into XLC and XLY. Rotation label: AI hardware fading / high-to-low rotation.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call ratio 1.31 (elevated protection). QQQ put-call ratio 0.96 (neutral). SPY IV skew +3.8% = slight put premium. VIX at 15.7 below its 20-day MA of 16.9 = growing complacency. SPY gamma flip at 760.0, spot at ~760? — dealer hedging could amplify any break.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +1.01% while XLE -0.79%: the disconnect signals energy stocks ignoring crude’s bounce, pressure from earnings rotation. Dollar -0.49% failed to boost GLD +0.05% — gold’s weakness persists.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 61 Greed. Top movers: PYPL +17.20% vol 5.8x (on takeover report), BLK +6.63% vol 2.1x (earnings), PGR -9.43% vol 2.0x (insurance). 20d-momentum extremes: best sector XLC +1.55%, worst XLP -2.74%; best basket KWEB +3.13%, worst DRAM -6.26%. Watch: IBM -2.70% vol 3.8x — distribution signal in cloud tech; SOXX -2.23% vol 1.1x — breakdown in semis heavy volume; PYPL +17.20% vol 5.8x — whether takeover bid holds the gap.
